Q1: Are Christmas trees dissimilar to deciduous trees? Answer: YesChristmas trees are ever- green trees. Deciduous trees are tree that lose their leave. Thus, Christ- mas trees are not dissimi- lar to deciduous trees. So the answer is no.Christmas trees are conifer trees. Deciduous trees are trees that lose their leaves each year.Christmas trees, fir trees and pine trees are conif- erous trees. Deciduous trees are not conifer- ous.
Q2: Do frogs feel disgust? Answer: YesDisgust is a human emo- tion. There is no evidence that frogs feel emotions. Thus, frogs don't feel dis- gust.Scientists do not currently know whether frogs feel emotions.Frogs, lizards and snakes are animals. Ani- mals can feel emotions such as disgust.
Q3: Is the foot part of the metric system? Answer: NoThe metric system uses meters. 1 meter is about 3.3 feet. Thus, the foot is part of the metric system. So the answer is yes.The foot is not part of the metric system.The foot, inch and yard are Imperial units. Im- perial units are not part of the metric sys- tem.
